titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Paul Mariah Papers
titlestmt.author: by Brooke McManus

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.Describing Archives: A Content Standard

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday September 5, 2024 at 04:12

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of writings, correspondence, personal papers, printed material, a small amount of photographs, a sound recording, and other papers by or relating to American publisher, poet, and gay rights activist Paul Mariah.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 8 (sound recording): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Elysium Press on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2018.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into five series: I. Writings, 1929-1992. II. Correspondence, 1898–2002. III. Personal Papers, 1967-1988, undated. IV. Printed Material, 1937-2014. V. Sound Recording, 1976.

bioghist.head: Paul Mariah (1937-1996)
bioghist.p: Paul Mariah (born Paul Meredith Jones) was a publisher and poet prominent in the gay literary scene in San Francisco in the 1960s and 1970s. Born in Whittington, Illinois in 1937 to Ada Pearl Kelley and Herman Jones, Mariah graduated from Southern Illinois University in 1959 with a degree in theater. After graduation, Mariah briefly taught at a middle school before he was incarcerated in Menard Correctional Center in Illinois for three years. After his release from prison, he changed his name to Paul Mariah. In 1966, Mariah moved to San Francisco and became involved with gay rights activism, joining the Society of Individual Rights. He earned a Master's degree in English literature from San Francisco State College in 1969. During his graduate studies, Mariah was a personal secretary for writers Kay Boyle and Robert Duncan. In August 1969, Mariah and Richard Tagett founded ManRoot, a small press that published books and chapbooks by many notable writers of the San Francisco Renaissance and the Stonewall era, as well as twelve issues of ManRoot poetry magazine. As a poet, Mariah's work appeared in numerous publications, and he published two poetry collections, Personae Non Gratae (1971) and This Light Will Spread (1978).

Mariah was a project scheduler and research assistant for the Kinsey Institute for Sex Research's San Francisco Homosexual Study in 1969-1970 and was a member of the Council on Religion and the Homosexual, serving as its president in 1971. Mariah was also a counselor from 1972 to 1975, working with former prisoners. His partner of fifteen years, Kenneth Poff, an Army veteran and ManRoot's business manager, died in 1986. Mariah died in 1996.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Paul Mariah Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections [as they are acquired], and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of writings, correspondence, personal papers, printed material, a small amount of photographs, a sound recording, and other papers by or relating to American publisher, poet, and gay rights activist Paul Mariah.

Writings contain drafts of poetry and prose by Mariah, Helen Luster, and Hunce Voelcker, as well as journals kept by Mariah and notes on Mariah's poems by his partner Kenneth Poff. Correspondence generally concerns professional matters, such as Mariah's poetry submissions and ManRoot publications. Included are letters and cards from Andrew Bifrost, James Broughton, Allen Ginsberg, Mary Norbert Körte, Lynn Lonidier, Richard Tagett, and Voelcker. The collection also contains Mariah's correspondence with his mother, Ada Pearl Jones. Personal papers contain artwork, notebooks, calendars, a few writings, ephemera, and Poff's will. Also included are a copy of visitation rules for the Menard Correctional Center; materials concerning Mariah's involvement with the Council on Religion and the Homosexual and the Kinsey Institute's study on gay men and women in San Francisco in 1969-1970; and a file on "gay phemenology" with typescripts of articles and speeches by others about gay liberation, marriage, and mental health. Printed material contains ephemera related to readings, exhibitions, concerts, symposia, and conferences documenting the work and activism of gay, Black, and Indigenous poets, artists, and musicians, as well as materials related to gay advocacy groups such as One, Inc. and the Society for Individual Rights.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Paul Mariah Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

corpname.part: Council on Religion and the Homosexual (San Francisco, Calif.)
Kinsey Institute for Research in Sex, Gender, and Reproduction

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains poetry and other writings by Paul Mariah, Helen Luster, and Hunce Voelcker. Mariah's works include Dances with Dali and "One Mad Queen: Poems for Cavafy." Files related to Luster contain publicity photographs, drafts of Crystal (book seven of her "I(EE)" series), and an unpublished collection of correspondence with Allen Ginsberg. Also included are Mariah's notebooks and journals, which contain writings and notes on personal and professional matters, and a few poetry broadsides by authors associated with ManRoot, such as James Broughton.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by author or folder title.

did.unittitle: Eberhardt, Karen. Resume and poems submitted to Mariah

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Helen Luster (1913-1985) was a poet, editor, founder of the Los Angeles Poetry Center, and personal secretary to Allen Ginsberg at Naropa University for ten years. She assembled a large collection of Ginsberg's writings and planned to publish their correspondence as a book shortly before her death. Luster's poems were published in ManRoot , and she co-led poetry workshops with Paul Mariah. She named Mariah as executor of the Ginsberg literary collection and correspondence in her will. Luster also had an interest in parapsychology, which informed some of her research and writings.

did.unittitle: Ditto of complete text of "I(EE), Book VII Snake & Crystal" (including prose piece "Crystal" which must be revised for this book)
Luster photos [includes negatives and publicity prints, clipping, high school report card]
"Helen and Allen: Oracle Letters to Allen Ginsberg, 1963-1983," master, original, edited and footnoted copy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also contains photographs of Luster and Ginsberg and autograph manuscript notes.

did.unittitle: Retyped letters from Helen to A.G.
Extra photocopies of Alan [sic] G's letters -- used in book -- in master copy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copy of "Letter to Robert Duncan While Reading Bending the Bow."

did.unittitle: "Concordances with Dali," composition for San Francisco State College poetry course
"Poems: Metaphysical Caverns," San Francisco State College master's thesis

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poems and essays by Mariah, a few poetry broadsides, including one signed by James Broughton, and copies of published writings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drafts of poems by Mariah and reading notes by Ken Poff. Includes some printed ephemera and clipping of a review of ManRoot issue #11.

did.unittitle: "One Mad Queen: Poems for Cavafy," typed copy
"The New I," poetry collection, typescript (photocopy), corrected
"Rivers & Bars," printed poem, preliminary mockup of ManRoot issue
Odes to a Public Body [poetry journal]
A working notebook, journal -- etc. Sept 1973 --
The Leathern Look [journal containing writings on various topics, reading notes]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: "An opera about going to the opera," most pages blank.

did.unittitle: Planthold, Mildred A. "Marjorie's Gift: a play in one act"
Voelcker, Hunce. "Bridge," typed copy inscribed to Paul Mariah on the title page

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of professional and personal correspondence of Paul Mariah. Correspondents include ManRoot cofounder Richard Tagett, poet and filmmaker James Broughton and artist Joel Singer, Beat poets Allen Ginsberg and Mary Norbert Körte, poet and musician Lynn Lonidier, and publishers and editors such as Andrew Bifrost from Mouth of the Dragon and Louise Simons from Painted Bride Quarterly . Also present are a number of poetry submissions by Mariah and correspondence regarding Vector magazine, published by the Society of Individual Rights. The series additionally contains Jones and Kelley family correspondence, including letters from Mariah to his mother, and a small amount of third party correspondence, primarily related to the work of Helen Luster.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by correspondent or folder title, with first name only, unidentified, and third party correspondence filed at the end of the sequence.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Correspondence can also be found in Series I. Writings and Series III. Personal Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Primarily related to final issue of ManRoot , titled "The Complete Poems of Jean Genet," with proofs of the cover and poems.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Hicks was incarcerated in Cook County Jail in Chicago.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with Mariah's sister Mary Alice Sims and brother George W. Jones, and a note and handmade booklet with flowers pressed in from Louella, presumably a relative, in 1898.

did.unittitle: Montague, Tony, with translations of René Char by Montague

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence related to Helen Luster's estate.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poetry submissions by Mariah, as well as correspondence with colleagues, family, and friends, writings, ephemera, and other papers formerly enclosed in an unmarked binder. Materials were removed from the binder and rehoused in folders by library staff during processing.

did.unittitle: Related to Diana (poetry collection by Mariah), Vector magazine
Bell, John S. (CERN) and Helen Luster
Heintz, William F. to Mary Alice Sims (Mariah's sister)
Luster, Helen related to quantum theory & ESP

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence, printed material, biographical notes on Luster, largely related to John Bell and Nick Herbert's book Quantum Reality .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of artwork, calendars, a journal, notecards related to church correspondence, a copy of Kenneth Poff's will, a visitor request form and outline of rules for visiting Menard Correctional Center, and other papers. Included are materials documenting gay activism, the Council on Religion and the Homosexual, and Mariah's work at the Kinsey Institute. The series also contains inventories of Helen Luster's collection of Allen Ginsberg writings and correspondence; Mariah was named in her will as executor of the Ginsberg materials after her death.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs, notes, and ephemera are enclosed.

did.unittitle: Council on Religion and the Homosexual, typed outline of advocacy actions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence, schedules and ephemera, and writings on gay marriage, mental health and gay liberation, and women's colleges. Among the authors and correspondents are Sally Gearheart, Robert W. Anderson, and Ronald D. Lee.

did.unittitle: Helen Luster collection of Allen Ginsberg, inventory
[Helen Luster] Allen Ginsberg Collection: Catalogue of Items as of March 17, 1984

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Diary entries and some notes on professional matters.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs, notecards, lists related to ManRoot , poems, and several pages of interview contacts, mostly likely for Mariah's work as an interviewer and scheduler with the Kinsey Institute's San Francisco study.

did.unittitle: [Kappa Teatron] Frat ΦƐΚ / Address & initiates listing
Menard Correctional Center visiting rules and procedures
"Men on Wood" by Robert Berner, pen or ink drawings on wooden squares

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Berner provided many of the illustrations featured in ManRoot magazine and designed covers of books printed by ManRoot.

did.unittitle: Paul's church correspondence / index / bibliography [alphabetical index cards]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of broadsides, fliers, pamphlets, catalogs, serials, tear sheets, clippings, and other printed material. The bulk of broadsides and fliers advertise poetry readings, concerts, plays, fundraising events, and seminars. Included are ephemera relating to the Society for Individual Rights; Gay Pride Week 1973 in Chicago; a concert by the West Coast Gangster Choir; "I am the Sun," an event featuring poet Maurice Kenny (Mohawk), a contributor to ManRoot magazine; and Last Summer at Bluefish Cove (1980), one of the first mainstream lesbian Broadway plays.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Printed material can also be found in Series I. Writings, Series II. Correspondence, and Series III. Personal Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes a few pamphlets related to gay rights issues.

did.unittitle: Committee of Small Magazine Editors and Publishers conference - poster and fliers
Ephemera related to criminal justice and prison reform

title.part: Gay Halloween Cruise News & World Report

did.unittitle: Kohoutek Celebration of Consciousness program, annotated
ManRoot - broadsides, fliers, press releases, and catalogs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Newspaper produced by incarcerees at the Menard Correctional Center.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Inscribed to Mariah.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes clippings and photocopy of "A Modest Proposal for Gay People's Liberation."

did.unittitle: Tear sheets from Saturday Review and New Republic
Unitarian-Universalist Gay Caucus of San Francisco News

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One 33⅓ rpm vinyl record enclosed with Jack Micheline's Purple Submarine .

did.unittitle: Micheline, Jack. Side One: "Tigers in the Sky" / Side Two: Songs and Poems Read by Jack Micheline

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
